title: Laȝamon’s Brut (Otho version)
summary: The repository XML represents the Otho version of Laȝamon's Brut, not an undifferentiated text of both surviving versions. Its primary path is General Corpus LayOtho.lineated.xml → the 1993 U-M CME item LayOtho → Oxford Text Archive deposit 1682's Otho payload → the August 1988 OTA 0085 Otho stream → G. L. Brook and R. F. Leslie's 1963-1978 EETS Otho text → British Library Cotton MS Otho C XIII. Character-normalized comparison matches all 14,560 legacy and current OTA rows; U-M differs substantively at only row 7574 after page-milestone whitespace is removed. The electronic convention also preserves selected now-illegible readings from Madden's 1847 parallel edition. Cotton Otho C XIII is a fire-damaged 144-folio single-work manuscript with no complete current public facsimile found; the British Library catalog and reproduction route are recorded without claiming nonexistence. Madden's complete three-volume edition scans, its selected Otho plate, OTA deliverables, Brook-Leslie metadata, the current full Caligula comparison facsimile, rights assertions, and cache fixity remain separate layers.
review_notes: Primary path: repository XML → U-M LayOtho → OTA 1682 Otho payload → OTA 0085 Otho stream → Brook-Leslie Otho stream → Cotton Otho C XIII.
All 14,560 legacy 0085 Otho rows match current 1682 after the documented character and delimiter conversion; 14,559 of 14,560 U-M rows then match after milestone whitespace normalization.
Madden 1847 supplies only the explicitly parenthesized now-illegible readings and a selected manuscript plate; neither is a whole-manuscript facsimile or whole-text base.

The complete Caligula IIIF object is a collateral witness to a different version. Its exact ff. 3r-194v / canvas 15-398 mapping is explicitly labeled as a collateral-version work portion, never as the target Otho manuscript.
No complete current public Otho facsimile was verified. A mediated whole-object photographic request, provider quote, and written reuse terms remain necessary.

agents.name: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for LayOtho.lineated
notes: The pinned XML file is 891,266 bytes.
It contains 14,560 consecutively numbered L rows, 146 supplied navigation groups, and 412 source-edition page milestones.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME digital text LayOtho
entities.description: The public CME presentation and structured XML transformation of the Otho stream. It numbers 14,560 L rows, supplies 146 100-line navigation groups and 412 Brook-Leslie page milestones, and converts the OTA custom-character notation to Unicode and normalized delimiters.
notes: The header says front and back matter, editorial notes, and printed illustrations from the source edition were omitted.

After milestone whitespace normalization, 14,559 of 14,560 U-M rows match current OTA 1682 Otho. At row 7574 U-M has ‘Þus endede par mid mochele wowe Vortiger.’ while OTA has ‘Þus endede þar; mid mochele wowe Vortiger.’; the responsible correction or corruption stage is undocumented.

entities.label: Oxford Text Archive deposit 1682, Otho payload
entities.description: The current SGML-like Otho stream in OTA deposit 1682. It contains 14,560 l rows and compact symbols for manuscript line breaks, Caligula-only additions, loss, editorial supply, marginal or interlinear additions, and readings recovered from Madden 1847.
notes: The allzip deliverable contains exactly the separate laycal-1682.txt and layotho-1682.txt payloads; the TEI header is a separate download.
The Otho payload is the upstream text stream for LayOtho. The Caligula payload is collateral and must not be merged into Otho wording.

entities.label: Oxford Text Archive item 0085, legacy Otho stream
entities.description: The earlier plain-text Otho stream, with an ASCII-era custom-character convention documented in an August 1988 encoding note. After the documented character and delimiter mappings, its 14,560 text rows match the current OTA 1682 Otho payload exactly.
notes: The current OTA item marks 0085 as replaced by 1682. The transformation changes character and delimiter representation without changing the 14,560 aligned text rows under the audited mapping.

entities.label: G. L. Brook and R. F. Leslie, eds., Laȝamon: Brut (1963-1978)

bibliographic.citation: Laȝamon: Brut, ed. G. L. Brook and R. F. Leslie, 2 vols., Early English Text Society, Original Series 250 and 277 (London: published for the Early English Text Society by Oxford University Press, 1963-1978).
bibliographic.title: Laȝamon: Brut
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 250, 277
bibliographic.publisher: Published for the Early English Text Society by Oxford University Press

entities.description: The immediate two-volume source edition, presenting distinct Caligula and Otho streams from Cotton Caligula A IX and Cotton Otho C XIII. The target electronic payload selects its Otho stream and excludes the edition's front/back matter, notes, and illustrations.
notes: The exact introductions and apparatus remain controlled-access; the public volume-1 metadata does not reveal every normalization, supply, or collation rule.
The XML says printed illustrations were not represented electronically. Their exact inventory requires inspection of both print volumes.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Otho C XIII

date_statements.label: manuscript production
date_statements.value: Fourth quarter of the thirteenth century (1275-1299)

physical_description.format: Manuscript of the Otho version of Laȝamon's Brut
notes: Leaves were damaged in the 1731 Cotton Library fire; the manuscript was rebound by the British Museum in 1962.

The current catalog records 144 folios. Madden reported 145 surviving leaves in 1847 and discussed still earlier 154/155-folio counts; these are dated historical states, not silently reconciled with present foliation.
Madden described one hand, double columns, 38 lines, colored initials, severe damage in the first fifty leaves, and additional missing or injured text.

entities.description: The surviving witness of the later abridged and reworked Otho version. It is an English parchment manuscript of the fourth quarter of the thirteenth century, extensively damaged in the 1731 Cotton Library fire. The exact current pencil-folio crosswalk to the electronic rows awaits a complete institutional surrogate.
notes: The official catalog describes this as one item and gives no current digitised-content link. That dated negative finding does not prove that private photography, microfilm, or unindexed legacy images do not exist.

entities.label: Frederic Madden, ed., Laȝamons Brut, or Chronicle of Britain (1847)

bibliographic.citation: Laȝamons Brut, or Chronicle of Britain: A Poetical Semi-Saxon Paraphrase of the Brut of Wace, ed. Frederic Madden, 3 vols. (London: Society of Antiquaries of London, 1847).
bibliographic.title: Laȝamons Brut, or Chronicle of Britain

entities.description: Madden's three-volume parallel presentation of the Caligula and Otho texts with literal translation, notes, glossary, and a selected Otho manuscript plate. The later electronic convention attributes single-parenthesized now-illegible readings specifically to this edition.
notes: Volume 1's preface calls Otho an abbreviated recension and gives a dated account of its 1731 fire damage, restoration, historical folio counts, missing leaves, and damaged text.
The selected Otho plate appears at PDF page 47 / printed preface page xxxviii in the cached volume 1. It is not a complete facsimile and its exact current folio was not verified.

entities.label: Internet Archive / Google scans of Madden's 1847 edition
entities.description: Three original Google Books PDF derivatives, together reproducing all three Madden volumes. They retain the parallel Otho/Caligula text, translation, notes, glossary, preface, and volume 1's selected Otho manuscript plate.
entities.reproduction_format: Three PDF page-image volumes, 512, 661, and 677 pages

entities.label: Madden 1847 selected facsimile plate of Cotton Otho C XIII
entities.description: A single printed facsimile plate selected by Madden from a comparatively well-preserved portion of the fire-damaged Otho manuscript, reproduced in volume 1 at printed preface page xxxviii / cached PDF page 47. The plate shows only a narrow, contracted manuscript specimen; its exact present folio was not verified.
entities.reproduction_format: One nineteenth-century printed plate embedded in volume 1
notes: Complete access to the scanned printed volume makes this selected plate viewable; it does not become a complete manuscript facsimile.

entities.label: Internet Archive controlled scan of Brook-Leslie volume 1
entities.description: A restricted controlled-digital-lending reproduction of the 1963 first volume. Only the public item metadata is cached; no private, encrypted, or loan-gated PDF was downloaded.

entities.label: Oxford Text Archive deposit 1682, Caligula payload
entities.description: The separate Caligula-version payload in the same OTA 1682 deposit. It is retained as a collateral parallel stream for version comparison, not as an upstream source of every Otho reading.
notes: The complete cached deposit includes this file, but the target repository artifact selects layotho-1682.txt rather than laycal-1682.txt.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Caligula A IX

date_statements.label: manuscript production
date_statements.value: Second half of the thirteenth century (1250-1299)

historical_designations: British Museum MS Cotton Caligula A.IX

physical_description.extent: 261 folios, with two modern endleaves and flyleaves; Laȝamon's Brut at ff. 3r-194v
physical_description.dimensions: approximately 215 × 150 mm; text space approximately 170 × 115 mm
physical_description.format: Composite codex written in two columns
notes: Origin assigned to England, south-west Midlands.
The Brut is the most complete surviving copy and opens at f. 3r with the red rubric ‘Incipit historia brutonum.’
The codex also contains unrelated Middle English and Anglo-Norman works outside this work record.

entities.description: The longer, more conservative Caligula-version witness. It is collateral to the target Otho version and useful for version comparison, but it is not treated as an unrestricted source of Otho wording.

entities.label: British Library complete IIIF surrogate of Cotton MS Caligula A IX
entities.description: A complete 543-canvas digital surrogate of the Caligula composite codex. The different Caligula version occupies ff. 3r-194v / canvases 15-398. This whole-codex cache is a collateral comparison source, not a facsimile of Cotton Otho C XIII.

physical_description.format: IIIF Presentation 3 manifest with JPEG image services
notes: 542 images were obtained at the requested 1200-pixel-width profile; the spine used the provider's full-size fallback and the inventory records both requested and effective URLs.

assertion.notes: Identifiers, header, structure, and text align; the exact repository transfer event is undocumented.
U-M restructures the payload, inserts page milestones and supplied divisions, and has one substantive normalized row difference.
After applying the documented legacy character and delimiter mapping, all 14,560 aligned rows match exactly.

object.description: Otho textual stream across both Brook-Leslie volumes

assertion.notes: The exact normalization and passage-level treatment require the edition introductions and apparatus.
This separate stream is contextual and is not in the target Otho primary path.
This relation documents the parallel edition column, not a claim that Caligula is the Otho base witness.

subject.description: Single-parenthesized readings no longer legible in the manuscript

assertion.notes: This is passage-scoped and does not make Madden the base for the whole Otho stream.

subject.description: Otho column of Madden's parallel edition

object.description: One narrow, contracted specimen from the fire-damaged Otho manuscript; exact current folio unverified
locators: Madden vol. 1, printed preface p. xxxviii; cached PDF p. 47

assertion.notes: The plate is visibly an Otho fragment reproduction, but a reliable current folio crosswalk was not established.

scope.notes: The collateral Caligula Brut is at ff. 3r-194v / canvases 15-398; these are comparison locators, not an Otho work portion.

notes: The cached file is the complete published volume and also contains the parallel Caligula column, literal translation, and editorial matter. Madden line numbers organize the parallel edition; they are not a complete intrinsic Otho line count.

access.access_method: Open the complete volume 1 PDF at PDF page 47, corresponding to printed prefatory page xxxviii.

local_copies.label: Madden volume 1 containing the selected Otho manuscript plate

work_portion.label: Selected narrow manuscript specimen from Cotton Otho C XIII
notes: Madden calls this a favorable specimen from the best-preserved portion of the volume; visual inspection shows a narrow, contracted strip rather than a complete leaf view. Exact current folio unverified.
The local file is a complete scan of Madden volume 1, but its embedded manuscript-image coverage is only this selected Otho plate.

access.access_method: Consult the current Archives and Manuscripts catalog record.
Restrictions apply. Register for a Reader Pass, request the shelfmark through the online form, and consult British Library staff before travel; manuscripts and archives are consulted in London.
No current digitised-content link or complete public facsimile was verified; ask the British Library whether legacy photography, microfilm, or a reproduction order is available.
access.format: Potential commissioned images or legacy surrogate

access.access_method: Submit the online Imaging Services order for Cotton MS Otho C XIII and request color expert photography of the complete present physical object: both boards/covers, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, fragments, edges if offered, and every surviving manuscript leaf. Give the work-to-folio crosswalk separately after institutional confirmation.

local_copies.label: British Library digitisation and reproduction price list
notes: The exact two-page provider PDF was retained byte-for-byte. qpdf opens it and reports a valid page tree but warns about recoverable linearization/shared-hint offsets; pdftotext extracts both pages successfully, so no repair was made.

Published 2025/26 rates include photographic TIFF at £27.70 for the first image, £7.25 per subsequent image, and £58.19 per complex-handling image. A fragile 144-folio whole-object project may fall outside standard rates and requires an institutional quote.

access.contact: permissions@bl.uk (current Imaging Services terms); licensing@bl.uk (digitisation page)
access.format: Commissioned color photography or an available surrogate; exact format and coverage subject to conservation review and quote
notes: The service says original manuscripts cannot be scanned; rare or precious items may require expert photography. If a surrogate or microfilm is used, explicitly request color because surrogate scans may be black and white.
The current digitisation page and January 2026 Imaging Services terms list different permission email aliases; preserve the provider's written response and controlling terms with any delivered files.

access.access_method: Download the separate Caligula payload directly or obtain it inside the complete two-file deposit.

locators: 958,405 bytes within the complete two-file deposit
notes: This portion belongs to the separately modeled Caligula version and is retained for comparison only.

access.access_method: Consult the official manuscript catalog for the collateral Caligula witness and its ff. 3r-194v work range.
Use the British Library IIIF viewer or Presentation 3 manifest. A complete local research bundle preserves the provider manifest, inventory, and all canvases.

local_copies.label: British Library Cotton MS Caligula A IX complete facsimile
notes: ZIP contains the exact provider manifest, an exact-source inventory, and all 543 canvas images.
542 image requests used the supported 1200-pixel-width profile; the spine's requested 1200-pixel URL was rejected and the effective full-size fallback is preserved in the inventory.
The collateral Caligula Brut occupies ff. 3r-194v / canvases 15-398; the required work_portion object below labels that different version explicitly and does not describe Cotton Otho C XIII.

work_portion.label: Collateral Caligula version of Laȝamon's Brut; not the target Otho version
locators: Cotton MS Caligula A IX, ff. 3r-194v
notes: This mapping associates the complete source cache with the separately modeled Caligula-version comparison relation. Cotton Otho C XIII is a different physical manuscript with no public complete facsimile found.
The ignored cache is a research copy of a different version's complete codex and is not itself permission to redistribute British Library photographs.

rights.component: Current U-M LayOtho digital-text presentation and represented materials
rights.basis: The current item page states that the materials are in the public domain and displays the DPLA No Copyright-United States statement. The scope of that display over independently protectable markup and metadata is not separately explained.
notes: A United States no-copyright statement is not a worldwide open license and does not license manuscript photographs or the modern source edition.

rights.component: Legacy availability notice embedded in the 1993 U-M XML header
rights.basis: The checked-in U-M header says the text was made available through OTA ‘for personal scholarly use only.’ This historical restriction conflicts in scope with the current U-M No Copyright-United States display and current OTA CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 deposit license; no item-specific reconciliation was found.
notes: The notice is preserved as historically explicit evidence, not silently treated as revoked or as controlling unrelated manuscript images and editions.

rights.basis: The Otho payload belongs to the same current OTA deposit distributed under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 3.0 Unported.
The current OTA item record for 0085 distributes the legacy item under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 3.0 Unported.
notes: This current deposit-layer statement does not settle the Brook-Leslie editorial layer or medieval text independently.

rights.basis: The current OTA record and public TEI header distribute deposit 1682 under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 3.0 Unported.
notes: The noncommercial restriction matters for commercial/POD workflows. This deposit-layer license is not transferred to Brook-Leslie page images, the manuscripts, or BL photographs.

rights.component: Checked-in XML serialization, transcription, and modern encoded labor
rights.basis: The pinned CME submodule carries an Unlicense worded for software, while the General Corpus README separately licenses code under GPL-3.0 and non-public-domain output under CC BY-SA 4.0. The imported XML also preserves an older personal-scholarly-use notice, and its identified OTA source now has CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 terms. No file-specific statement reconciles those layers, so no single repository license is asserted as controlling every textual and markup component.
notes: The medieval text's age does not automatically resolve rights in modern editorial selection, apparatus, markup, or repository-created output.

rights.component: Modern editorial text, apparatus, introductions, notes, and facsimile matter in the 1963 and 1978 volumes
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1963 and 1978 and no open license or public-domain assertion for its modern editorial contributions was found. The available volume-1 scan is controlled/print-disabled rather than openly downloadable.
notes: No exact term expiry is inferred because relevant authorship, employer, and publication facts were not fully established.

rights.component: Controlled-digital-lending scan and page-image derivatives for Brook-Leslie volume 1
rights.basis: Internet Archive metadata marks the item inlibrary/printdisabled and its ordinary PDF private, while supplying no open license or rights field.
notes: Only public metadata is cached. Borrowing access does not authorize repository redistribution of the scan.

rights.component: Underlying 1847 publication text, translation, notes, and glossary
rights.basis: The three-volume edition was published in 1847 and is historical public-domain material in the United States.

rights.component: Google-produced PDF reproductions and Internet Archive delivery layer
rights.basis: The underlying 1847 books are public-domain material in the United States. Each delivered PDF begins with Google's own usage-guidelines page, which requests personal noncommercial use, no automated querying, retention of the Google watermark/attribution, and independent attention to country-specific copyright status. The Internet Archive item metadata supplies no separate scan-layer open license.
notes: The embedded guidance is recorded as a provider/delivery-file layer and not misstated as copyright in Madden's historical edition.
Public download availability is not converted into an unrestricted license for the provider-specific PDF reproductions.

rights.basis: The plate was published as part of Madden's 1847 edition.
notes: This does not automatically license Internet Archive or Google delivery-layer files in every jurisdiction.

rights.component: Google-produced PDF reproduction carrying the selected plate
rights.basis: The underlying 1847 plate is public-domain material in the United States, while the containing Google-produced PDF begins with usage guidance requesting personal noncommercial use, no automated querying, and retention of the Google watermark/attribution. The Internet Archive metadata provides no separate scan-layer open license.
notes: The Google request governs the delivered-file/provider layer recorded here; it is not treated as renewed copyright in the 1847 plate.

rights.component: Underlying medieval text and manuscript content, excluding any modern photography and catalog text
rights.basis: The represented manuscript and text were produced in the thirteenth century. This status is separate from ownership, access restrictions, fire-damage documentation, photography, and provider terms.

rights.component: Any commissioned British Library photographs, scans from surrogates, delivery files, and reproduction rights
rights.jurisdiction: Provider contract and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: British Library Imaging Services terms updated January 2026 say supplied material may be subject to rights and must not be copied or reproduced without written British Library permission; the service page separately directs reproduction enquiries to licensing staff.
notes: A quote or delivery does not by itself authorize Git redistribution, research publication, or commercial/print-on-demand use. Preserve the written permission and exact covered files.

rights.component: Underlying medieval text and manuscript content, excluding modern photography and catalog text
rights.basis: The represented manuscript and text were produced in the thirteenth century. This status is separate from ownership, physical access, photography, and provider terms.

rights.component: British Library manuscript photographs and IIIF presentation
rights.jurisdiction: Most countries other than the United Kingdom
rights.basis: The exact IIIF manifest's Usage terms say ‘Public Domain in most countries other than the UK.’ Its machine-actionable rights URL points to a now-missing BL help page, while current BL terms say that content may have item-specific public-domain, Creative Commons, or other conditions.
notes: This is an item-level public-domain assertion, not an open license. UK status and any provider conditions on repository redistribution or commercial/POD use require written clarification; local caching does not supply that permission.

rights.component: British Library IIIF photographs in the United Kingdom and intended Git redistribution or commercial/print-on-demand reuse
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and intended provider-governed reuse
rights.basis: The exact manifest limits its public-domain statement to most countries other than the UK and points to a dead reuse-help URL. Current BL terms require users to follow item-specific conditions, and the digitisation service says reproduction images may be used as required only with proper permissions. No current item-specific permission for Git redistribution or commercial/POD use was found.
notes: Contact licensing@bl.uk and preserve a written response before publishing the cached image bundle or image-derived facsimiles in the repository or commercial outputs.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Two substantially different manuscript versions, Caligula and Otho
editorial_practices.representation: Separate Caligula and Otho textual streams across the same two-volume edition
editorial_practices.description: Brook and Leslie identify both manuscripts and present distinct streams rather than silently conflating the Otho rewriting into one undifferentiated text. Exact apparatus and passage-level use require the controlled edition.
notes: This record does not infer that every Otho supply derives from Caligula.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Brook-Leslie Otho stream, apparatus, front/back matter, notes, and illustrations
editorial_practices.representation: 14,560 text rows with compact inline symbols; footnotes and printed non-text matter omitted
editorial_practices.description: The electronic note preserves `%` for manuscript line breaks, `=` and `*` for Caligula-only line categories, plus signs for missing letters, double parentheses for interlinear/marginal additions, brackets for editorial supplies, and single parentheses for Madden-derived readings no longer legible.

editorial_practices.source_feature: ASCII-era custom characters and delimiters in OTA 0085
editorial_practices.representation: SGML entities, Unicode-ready character forms, brackets, and doubled parentheses in OTA 1682
editorial_practices.description: A documented normalization maps thorn, yogh, ash, eth, supplied-reading quotes, and addition delimiters. Under that mapping all 14,560 legacy and current Otho rows match exactly.

editorial_practices.source_feature: OTA Otho payload and Brook-Leslie printed-page sequence
editorial_practices.representation: 14,560 numbered L elements in 146 supplied 100-line DIV1 units with 412 PB milestones
editorial_practices.description: U-M made the Otho stream browseable by supplying navigation groups and page milestones and normalizing characters and delimiters. These structures are editorial navigation, not manuscript chapters.
notes: The U-M header contains one inconsistent shorthand line saying `=` indicates missing letters, while the fuller OTA note and actual Otho payload use plus signs for missing letters and `=` for Caligula-only lines. Interpret symbols from the exact stream and full convention note.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Two-volume printed edition with front matter, back matter, editorial notes, and illustrations
editorial_practices.representation: Otho verse rows and selected pagination retained; front/back matter, editorial notes, and illustrations omitted
editorial_practices.description: The explicit omission boundary prevents the electronic item from being described as a complete digital reproduction of Brook and Leslie.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Otho passages no longer legible when the later edition/encoding was prepared
editorial_practices.representation: Madden-derived readings enclosed in single parentheses
editorial_practices.description: The electronic convention creates a visible passage-scoped dependency on Madden's earlier transcription. It must not be generalized into a whole-text Madden base.

evidence.citation: General Corpus / CME source XML, CME/source/CME_from_OTA/LayOtho.lineated.xml.
evidence.summary: Primary local evidence for LayOtho and U-1682-C, the 1993 U-M date, Brook-Leslie source edition, explicit omissions, symbol definitions, Wace note, complete encoded Otho stream, supplied line groups, and page milestones.

evidence.citation: Target-scoped structural and locator audit of LayOtho.lineated.xml.
evidence.summary: Counts 14,560 consecutive numbered L rows, including 12,426 letter-bearing and 2,134 marker-only rows, 146 supplied line groups, 412 page milestones, three notes, and no authorial chapter hierarchy.

evidence.citation: Kyvero Press CME Git repository at commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: Pins the exact 891,266-byte submodule source, Git blob, commit, and path used for this record.

evidence.citation: Kyvero Press CME repository LICENSE at commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: The pinned submodule dedicates ‘software’ under the Unlicense. Its software wording does not expressly resolve imported transcription, scholarly-edition, or manuscript-image rights.

evidence.citation: General Corpus README license section and CC BY-SA 4.0 legal text.
evidence.summary: The project states code is GPL-3.0 and output other than public-domain material is CC BY-SA 4.0, while encouraging commercial use. It does not expressly reconcile imported U-M/OTA XML, modern source-edition rights, or manuscript-image terms.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, LayOtho, Layamon's Brut (British Museum Ms. Cotton Otho C.XIII).
evidence.summary: Official provider evidence for the Otho title, Laȝamon attribution, Brook-Leslie publication statement, OTA U-1682-C, 14,560-row contents navigation, public access, public-domain statement, and No Copyright-United States display.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive, item 1682, Layamon's Brut.
evidence.summary: Current official deposit record for the 3 April 1992 issue, separate Caligula and Otho files, relationship to the legacy plain-text item, public bitstreams, and CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 license. The record's legacy relation metadata is internally inconsistent between 0082 and 0085, so exact stream continuity is supported separately by byte-level content comparison.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive 1682 public TEI header, header1682.xml.
evidence.summary: Complete valid XML header supporting the Brook-Leslie source edition, 1190-1215 creation metadata, two-file laycal/layotho map, 1992 deposit date, 1997-2003 header revisions, and CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 deposit-layer license. Current and legacy header bitstreams were byte-identical.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive 1682 complete Otho payload, layotho-1682.txt.
evidence.summary: Complete 732,741-byte current Otho payload. Its note defines manuscript/addition/supply conventions and identifies single-parenthesized now-illegible readings as Madden-derived. It is the direct OTA text stream compared with U-M and legacy 0085.

evidence.citation: Fixity and container audit of the complete OTA 1682 allzip deliverable.
evidence.summary: The 1,691,767-byte ZIP passes integrity testing and contains exactly laycal-1682.txt (958,405 bytes) and layotho-1682.txt (732,741 bytes; SHA-256 5a596af8b96a0f4fa899737ab09576a378adc34787ba190b0f2d2494dc1fd724). The TEI header is a separate public bitstream.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive, item 0085, legacy plain-text Layamon's Brut.
evidence.locator: item metadata, files, replacement notice, license
evidence.summary: Official legacy-item record identifying public files, current replacement by 1682, and CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 deposit-layer terms.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive item 0085 encoding note, doc-0085.txt.
evidence.summary: Complete 880-byte August 1988 note defining custom thorn, ash, yogh, and eth characters; manuscript line breaks; Caligula-only line markers; damage; marginal/interlinear additions; editorial supplies; and Madden-derived now-illegible readings.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive item 0085 legacy Otho payload, otho-0085.txt.
evidence.summary: Complete 587,682-byte legacy Otho stream copied from Brook-Leslie with its ASCII-era character and damage conventions.

evidence.citation: Computational row alignment of cached OTA 0085 Otho, OTA 1682 Otho, and pinned U-M/General Corpus XML.
evidence.summary: After applying the documented legacy character mappings and converting supplied-reading quotes to brackets and addition angles to double parentheses, all 14,560 0085 rows match 1682 exactly. Against U-M, 14,559 rows match after removing whitespace inserted around PB milestones. The single substantive difference is row 7574: OTA has ‘Þus endede þar; mid mochele wowe Vortiger.’ and U-M has ‘Þus endede par mid mochele wowe Vortiger.’
notes: The comparison establishes stream continuity and one U-M variation; it does not identify who made that variation.

evidence.citation: CiNii Books, Laȝamon, Brut: edited from British Museum MS Cotton Caligula A. IX and British Museum MS Cotton Otho C. XIII, NCID BA02706965.
evidence.locator: bibliographic information; author/editor authorities; volume note
evidence.summary: Confirms G. L. Brook and R. F. Leslie, both named manuscripts, two EETS Original Series volumes 250 and 277, publication in 1963-1978, and the line-range split at 1-8020 and 8021-end.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive public item metadata for la3amonbrut0000glbr, Brook-Leslie volume 1 (1963).
evidence.summary: Identifies the 1963 Brook-Leslie volume and controlled collections internetarchivebooks/inlibrary/printdisabled; the ordinary PDF is private and no open license or rights field is supplied. Only this public metadata was downloaded.

evidence.citation: Frederic Madden, ed., Laȝamons Brut, or Chronicle of Britain, vol. 1 (London: Society of Antiquaries, 1847), Internet Archive original Google PDF.
evidence.locator: opening Google public-domain and usage-guidelines pages; preface pp. xvii-xix and xxxviii; PDF p. 47 selected Otho plate; parallel text through Madden line 10229
evidence.summary: Complete clean 512-page scan. Its opening Google pages identify the historical book as public domain while requesting personal noncommercial use, no automated querying, retention of attribution/watermark, and jurisdiction-specific legal review. Madden calls Otho an abbreviated recension, recounts its pre- and post-fire folio states, missing leaves and damage, describes its script/layout, estimates losses, prints the opening parallel text, and includes at PDF p. 47 / printed preface p. xxxviii a narrow facsimile specimen selected from what he calls the best-preserved portion of the volume.

evidence.citation: Frederic Madden, ed., Laȝamons Brut, or Chronicle of Britain, vol. 2 (London: Society of Antiquaries, 1847), Internet Archive original Google PDF.
evidence.locator: opening Google usage-guidelines pages; parallel text from Madden line 10230 to before line 25275
evidence.summary: Complete clean 661-page scan preserving the continuing parallel Otho and Caligula columns and literal translation; it repeats the Google public-domain and personal-noncommercial-use guidance recorded for volume 1.

evidence.citation: Frederic Madden, ed., Laȝamons Brut, or Chronicle of Britain, vol. 3 (London: Society of Antiquaries, 1847), Internet Archive original Google PDF.
evidence.locator: opening Google usage-guidelines pages; parallel text from Madden line 25275; notes and glossary
evidence.summary: Complete clean 677-page scan preserving the end of the parallel text and the edition's notes and glossary; it repeats the Google public-domain and personal-noncommercial-use guidance recorded for volume 1.

evidence.citation: British Library, Cotton MS Otho C XIII, Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue.
evidence.locator: whole item; physical characteristics; access links
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the imperfect Otho Brut, 1275-1299 date, parchment support, 144-folio extent, 305 × 245 mm dimensions, 1731 fire damage, 1962 binding, restricted physical access, and absence of a current digitised-content link.

evidence.citation: British Library, Cotton MS Caligula A IX, Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue.
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the current shelfmark, 1250-1299 date, south-west Midlands origin, parchment codex, 261-folio extent, dimensions, composite contents, Laȝamon and Areley Kings attribution, alliterative form, exact Brut folios, status as most complete surviving copy, and Reader Pass/request routes.

evidence.citation: British Library IIIF Presentation 3 manifest for Cotton MS Caligula A IX.
evidence.summary: Exact 543-canvas complete-codex manifest. It identifies the manuscript, British Library digitisation, work contents, and Usage terms ‘Public Domain in most countries other than the UK’; its rights URI now returns 404. Canvases 15 and 398 are f. 3r and f. 194v.

evidence.citation: Complete-object cache, fixity, inventory, and visual audit of the British Library Cotton MS Caligula A IX IIIF surrogate.
evidence.summary: The 301,950,000-byte ZIP passes integrity testing and contains the exact manifest, inventory, and all 543 listed JPEGs. Inventory order confirms front cover at 1, f. 3r at 15, f. 194v at 398, and spine at 543; 542 effective requests used `1200,` and the spine used a recorded `full` fallback. Visual inspection confirmed the illuminated f. 3r opening with ‘Incipit historia brutonum,’ the text-bearing f. 194v endpoint, and the spine.

evidence.citation: Nelson Goering, ‘Metrical Resolution in Early Middle English,’ in Prosody in Medieval English and Norse (British Academy / Oxford University Press, 2023), pp. 126-145.
evidence.summary: Open current scholarship supporting the two-version boundary, modern Brut title, manuscript-era title forms, conservative Caligula and later abridged/reworked Otho distinction, manuscript chronology, likely later-twelfth-century composition before 1216, Areley Kings/West Midlands association, long-line form, and direct availability of the Caligula facsimile.

evidence.citation: British Library, Terms of Use.
evidence.summary: Current provider terms explain that BL content may be public domain, Creative Commons licensed, or subject to other rights and conditions, and that item-specific markings govern. They do not replace the exact IIIF Usage statement or resolve the manifest's excluded UK jurisdiction.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation Services.
evidence.locator: online order, required manuscript details, original-manuscript photography, surrogate/color warning, permissions contact
evidence.summary: Official service page and exact online order route. It says original manuscripts cannot be scanned, rare originals may require expert photography, surrogate or microfilm scans may be black-and-white unless color is requested, and reproduction/publication requires appropriate permission.

evidence.citation: British Library, Imaging Services terms and conditions, updated January 2026.
evidence.locator: preservation, large-order pricing, copyright and reproductions
evidence.summary: Current special terms say fragile, large, or special orders may require individual agreement; supplied material may carry third-party rights; and customers must not copy or reproduce Imaging Services material without written British Library permission. The page lists permissions@bl.uk.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation and reproduction services price list, 2025/26 rates.
evidence.summary: Exact two-page provider price card: JPEG scan £15.25 first and £0.58 subsequent page; photographic TIFF £27.70 first and £7.25 subsequent page; complex handling £58.19 per image. Projects above £10,000 and nonstandard requirements require consultation. qpdf opens the file but reports recoverable linearization/shared-hint warnings; text extraction succeeds, and the original bytes were retained.

evidence.citation: Dated search for a complete current public digital facsimile of British Library Cotton MS Otho C XIII.
evidence.locator: exact catalog record and current public discovery routes
evidence.summary: No current digitised-content link, IIIF manifest, or complete public facsimile was verified as of 2026-07-15. This negative result is not evidence that private photography, microfilm, or unindexed legacy files do not exist.

open_questions.question: What exact base-text, collation, supply, normalization, and apparatus rules did Brook and Leslie use for the Otho stream, and what illustrations were omitted from the electronic text?
next_steps: Consult both printed volumes' introductions, apparatus, and illustration lists through a holding library or controlled borrowing.
Map each electronic symbol category to the edition's exact convention without assuming every Otho supply comes from Caligula.
Model each omitted manuscript plate separately after its target and extent are verified.

open_questions.question: Who transformed OTA Otho into the 1993 U-M item, from which exact source copy, and why does U-M row 7574 differ from current and legacy OTA?
next_steps: Ask U-M for item-level production metadata, source files, and correction history.
Compare row 7574 with Brook-Leslie and Madden before preferring either reading.
Do not silently correct the repository XML until the responsible layer is established.

open_questions.question: Can the British Library supply a complete current-object color facsimile, or identify a complete legacy microfilm or photographic surrogate, of Cotton MS Otho C XIII?
next_steps: Follow up through Imaging Services and Reference Services with shelfmark Cotton MS Otho C XIII.
Request all present surfaces—boards/covers, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, fragments, edges if offered, and every surviving leaf—not merely selected text pages.
After receipt, record exact delivery fixity and map the Otho text to current folios/canvases separately.

open_questions.question: What written British Library terms would govern private research storage, Git redistribution, scholarly publication, self-publishing, and commercial/print-on-demand reuse of any commissioned Otho images?
next_steps: Preserve the user's institutional response, quote, order confirmation, and exact terms with the delivered-file record.
Ask which permission contact and terms control, because the digitisation page and January 2026 Imaging Services terms list different email aliases.
Do not commit, redistribute, or include commissioned images in publication packages until written permission covers the exact use.

open_questions.question: How do Madden's historical 145-leaf state, the current catalog's 144 folios, missing leaves, and Brook-Leslie/OTA row sequence map to one another?
next_steps: Use a complete current surrogate and current pencil foliation as the physical baseline.
Transcribe Madden's missing-leaf discussion and compare Brook-Leslie page/line references.
Retain dated historical counts rather than forcing them into one invented continuous foliation.

open_questions.question: How should the 1993 personal-scholarly-use notice, current U-M No Copyright-United States display, OTA CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 licenses, CME software-worded Unlicense, and General Corpus output license be reconciled for redistribution of this exact XML?
next_steps: Request item-level clarification from U-M and OTA about transcription and markup.
Ask maintainers to define repository-license scope over imported XML.
Keep medieval text, Brook-Leslie edition, OTA streams, U-M encoding, repository serialization, and generated output separate until clarified.

open_questions.question: What current British Library terms govern redistribution or publication of the cached collateral Caligula IIIF photographs, especially in the United Kingdom?
next_steps: Ask the British Library to clarify the exact manifest's ‘Public Domain in most countries other than the UK’ statement and intended reuse.
Keep the complete cache private and Git-ignored pending clarification.
